text.title: Scope Management Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules — every scope statement must satisfy all five.

rule.statement: Scope statement MUST contain an explicit Out-of-Scope list with at least one item; absence is treated as 'no exclusions agreed'.
rule.rationale: Vague deliverable language plus no exclusions guarantees creep into adjacent expectations.

rule.statement: Every acceptance criterion MUST be testable (observable + measurable). Business-prose AC is rejected.
rule.rationale: Untestable AC produces disputes at handoff and silent slips.

rule.statement: Scope baseline MUST be signed by the named sponsor before WBS or schedule work begins.
rule.rationale: Unsigned scope is a moving target; downstream WBS/schedule investment is wasted.

rule.statement: RTM MUST cover every requirement; orphaned requirements (no AC) and orphaned AC (no requirement) are rejected.
rule.rationale: Orphans signal silent scope drift or untracked features.

rule.statement: Every change request MUST pass the change-control gate with cost + schedule impact analysis; absorbed-silently changes are rejected.
rule.rationale: Absorbed changes compound into overrun without visibility.
